response:
The total response time sums the individual database functions with their assigned query shares line  
Each database receives a portion of the total query load based on the given proportions line  
MySQL handles alpha times Q queries while Oracle handles beta times Q queries line  
Sybase processes the remaining queries which equal one minus alpha minus beta times Q line  
The total function combines these three distinct response time expressions into one formula line  
We substitute the specific query counts into each database response time equation line  
The resulting expression for total time depends on the variables alpha and beta line  
Optimization requires finding the values of alpha and beta that produce the minimum time line  
Numerical methods are needed because the function contains exponential and logarithmic terms line  
The constants provided allow us to calculate specific response times for any given distribution line  
We evaluate the function across a range of alpha and beta values to find the minimum line  
The search process identifies the optimal distribution that balances the load effectively line  
This approach ensures the system operates with the lowest possible average response time line  
The original constants define the behavior of each database under varying query loads line
